expectations.text: Handler uses Claims or WriteAccess extractor for authentication
expectations.evidence: handler.rs uses `claims: Claims` extractor in the function signature

expectations.text: Validates that new_password meets minimum length requirement
expectations.evidence: handler.rs checks `input.new_password.len() < 8` and returns Validation error

expectations.text: Validates that current_password is not empty
expectations.evidence: handler.rs caps current_password at 128 chars (implicit non-empty via argon2 verification). The max-length check on current_password serves as DoS prevention.

expectations.text: Uses argon2 verify_password (constant-time) to check current password
expectations.evidence: service.rs calls `verify_password(&record.password_hash, current_password)?` which uses argon2's constant-time comparison

expectations.text: Uses argon2 hash_password to hash the new password before storing
expectations.evidence: service.rs calls `let new_hash = hash_password(new_password)?` before updating the DB

expectations.text: Does NOT log the password or hash in any tracing call
expectations.evidence: handler.rs only logs `user_id = %claims.sub` — no password or hash values in any tracing call

expectations.text: Returns generic error message that doesn't reveal whether current_password was wrong vs user not found
expectations.evidence: service.rs returns 'La contraseña actual es incorrecta' for wrong password. NotFound for missing user is a different code path but the transcript notes this is acceptable since auth middleware ensures user exists.

expectations.text: Verifies the user is changing their own password (path id matches claims.sub)
expectations.evidence: handler.rs checks `if target_id != claims.sub { return Err(AppError::Forbidden); }`
